other_observers: none; first found at this location two years previously by Mona Hill
specific_location: along Yellow Jacket Creek, near the junction with Risley Canyon
description: the birds were small warblers, noticeably smaller than Yellow Warblers in the same tree. Their shape was similar but slightly more slender than the nearby Yellow Warblers, but with a slighter bill that was almost spike-like. The bill and legs were black.  The heads on the birds were pale gray, with a paler loral area. The back and wings were the same color on two of the birds, and on one bird (the juvie) the wings had two buffy wingbars. The underparts on all three bird was a paler gray, almost white, but with even paler (white) undertail coverts. I saw a brick-reddish rump on one of the birds, and on the juvie saw a buffy rump, but on the third bird did not see the rump well enough to tell color. The tails on all three birds was the same gray color as the back. I never saw any red coloring on the crown of any of the birds.Behaviors: The birds appeared to be a family group, and were foraging fairly high in a cottonwood along the creek. All three were somewhat hard to see.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
